{{Quote|The Zulirian Swordmasters remind me of many of the eccentric groups I covered while I was employed by the various news agencies in the Core: they are eager for attention.|Lieutenant Tura Raftican, Alliance to Restore the Republic|<ref name="A" />}}
'''Tura Raftican''' was an investigative [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] working for the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNews Network]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], she joined the [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Intelligence]] department of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], working under [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Arhul Hextrophon/Legends|Arhul Hextrophon]] and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]. Raftican eventually became a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] and wrote a report on the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]] and the threat that they presented to the Alliance. To perform her duties, Raftican made use of her background as a journalist.

==Biography==
Tura Raftican, a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]], worked as an investigative [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] for the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNews Network]]<ref name="A" /><ref name="R" /> and other news services from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. In this occupation, she covered the activities of several collectives seeking recognition through their work<ref name="A" /> and developed a vast network of contacts while doing this.<ref name="R" />

[[File:Task_Force_on_Alliance_Security2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Raftican (right) with Deeve, Bakki Sourthol, Tay Vanis and Anson Blazer.]]
Raftican joined [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|the Intelligence branch]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]]; her main job in the Alliance was to provide intelligence to Rebel forces,<ref name="A" /> using many of her contacts from her days as a journalist.<ref name="R" /> Among her main contacts in the Rebel Alliance were [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Arhul Hextrophon/Legends|Arhul Hextrophon]] and [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]]. After reaching the rank of lieutenant herself, Raftican worked with a team of Rebel [[Officer/Legends|officers]] that included [[Lieutenant Commander/Legends|Lieutenant Commanders]] [[Deeve]] and [[Bakki Sourthol]], Lieutenant [[Anson Blazer]], and Special Agent [[Tay Vanis]].<ref name="A" />

Raftican wrote a report for the [[Task Force on Alliance Security]] about the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]]. In it, she noted that the Swordmasters had been simple lovers of [[Datafile/Legends|datafiles]] before turning to fencing and that their move to swordplay was calculated to garner attention. Nevertheless, she cautioned that they deserved to be considered a threat, even though that was exactly what they were looking for.<ref name="A" />

==Skills and abilities==
Raftican was considered a hard worker, both as a journalist and as an intelligence agent. She was known for using journalism skills to obtain secret information and for her position as a prominent professional of the press. These abilities made her an impressively capable [[Spy/Legends|spy]].<ref name="A" />

==Equipment==
Raftican sometimes favored wearing [[Necklace/Legends|necklaces]] when she went outdoors.<ref name="A" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tura Raftican was first mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[roleplaying game]] book edited by [[Bill Smith]] and published by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]].<ref name="A" /> She later appeared in [[1998]] as a "recruitable character" in the computer game [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']].<ref name="R" />
